---
title: "Flow Analysis Template"
output: html_notebook
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r libraries}
library(flowCore)
library(flowWorkspace)
library(CytoML)
library(ggcyto)
library(openCyto)
library(FlowSOM)
library(uwot)
setwd("D:/Zhang Lab/MDS Project/srsf2_exp2")
```

```{r functions}
# 1. Import FlowJo workspace directly
ws <- open_flowjo_xml("my_workspace.wsp")
gs <- flowjo_to_gatingset(ws, name = "my_group")

# 2. Or start fresh from .fcs files
fcs <- read.flowSet(files = list.files("data/", pattern = ".fcs", full.names = TRUE))


# Check channels — Aurora will have an "Autofluorescence" channel
colnames(fcs[[1]])

# Exclude it from analysis/clustering if needed
channels_of_interest <- colnames(fcs[[1]])[!grepl("Autofluorescence|AF", 
                                                    colnames(fcs[[1]]))]

# 4. Transform (Logicle, similar to FlowJo's biexponential)
# estimateLogicle handles negatives well — preferred for Aurora
trans <- estimateLogicle(fcs[[1]], channels = channels_of_interest)
fcs_trans <- transform(fcs, trans)

# 5. Gating
gs <- GatingSet(fcs_trans)
gate_lymph <- polygonGate(filterId = "Lymphocytes",
                           "FSC-A" = c(50000, 200000, 200000, 50000),
                           "SSC-A" = c(10000, 10000, 80000, 80000))
add(gs, gate_lymph, parent = "root")
recompute(gs)

# 6. Visualize (like FlowJo plots)
autoplot(gs[[1]], gate = "Lymphocytes")

# 7. Get statistics
gs_pop_get_stats(gs, nodes = gs_get_pop_paths(gs))
```

```{r}
# Get all fcs files with their folder paths
fcs_files <- list.files("data/", pattern = "\\.fcs$", 
                         full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)

# Extract group name from folder name
groups <- basename(dirname(fcs_files))

# Load all files
cs <- load_cytoset_from_fcs(fcs_files)

# Assign group metadata
pData(cs)$group <- groups
pData(cs)
#>       name     group
#> 1  sample1      PBMC
#> 2  sample2      PBMC
#> 3  sample3     Tumor
#> 4  sample4     Tumor
#> 5  sample5        LN
#> 6  sample6        LN
```
